title: Tethricor HarnessConfig (harness.yaml)
description: Contract for a Tethricor configuration. The developer declares intent; the platform enforces security. Deployment target, egress/security, workload identity, and hardened image resolution are platform-injected and MUST NOT appear here (they are set by the CLI/webhook, not the developer).

type.description: A harness adapter name. Built-in adapters: hermes, pi, feynman, openhands, goose, opencode (`pi` and `feynman` share the Pi adapter). Not a closed set: any name registered as a HarnessAdapter (via the tethricor.harnesses entry point, or tethricor_runtime.adapters.register_harness) is also valid. Validated at generation/run time against the installed adapter registry, not statically here, so third-party harness plugins don't require a schema change.

version.description: Requested harness version. Resolved to a platform-controlled hardened image tag via the image manifest (developers cannot override the image itself).

routing_profile.description: v1 abstraction: an alias understood by the AgentGateway router. NOT provider credentials. Migrates to gateway model aliases in a later version.

direct_azure_openai.description: LOCAL TESTING ONLY escape hatch used when AgentGateway is unavailable locally. The CLI MUST strip/reject this for any non-local --target.

endpoint.description: Azure OpenAI resource endpoint, e.g. https://<resource>.openai.azure.com

skills.description: v1: free-form list of skill references (opaque strings). A future version will validate these against the live enterprise catalog.

mcp.description: MCP servers to attach, reached through AgentGateway.

servers.description: v1: free-form catalog references (opaque strings), not URLs.

profile.description: Agent-runtime sandbox profile. MUST be one of the values returned by the runtime's GET /v1/profiles (validated at generation time, not statically, because AGENT_RUNTIME_PROFILE_IMAGES can extend the set). Known values include: minimal, skills-minimal, git, node20, skill-security-runner, python312, rust, go, polyglot-dev.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-task execution timeout hint forwarded to the runtime.

provider.description: Sandbox provider name (plug-and-play backend). No implied default — choose explicitly (e.g. `remote-runtime` for any service implementing the sandbox execution contract, or an installed OSS provider like `microsandbox`/`e2b`). Validated at generation/run time against the installed provider registry (tethricor.discovery), not statically, because provider packages register via entry points.

source.description: Code IN: the repo cloned into the runtime session at create time (read-only clone token injected by the platform).

repo_url.description: Git repository URL to clone into the session.

ref.description: Git ref (branch, tag, or commit) to check out.

output.description: Code OUT: how changed files are returned. No git write-back in v1.

mode.description: zip-download (primary): zip of changed files fetched via the runtime artifact download endpoint. stdout-changed-files (fallback): base64 JSONL records over the SSE stdout channel.
